title: Conesus Lake

infobox.value: [Livingston County](/wiki/Livingston_County,_New_York "Livingston County, New York"),  [New York](/wiki/New_York_(state) "New York (state)"), United States

[Conesus Inlet](/w/index.php?title=Conesus_Inlet&action=edit&redlink=1 "Conesus Inlet (page does not exist)"), [Wilking Creek](/w/index.php?title=Wilking_Creek&action=edit&redlink=1 "Wilking Creek (page does not exist)"), [Denshore Gully](/w/index.php?title=Denshore_Gully&action=edit&redlink=1 "Denshore Gully (page does not exist)"), [Sand Point Gully](/w/index.php?title=Sand_Point_Gully&action=edit&redlink=1 "Sand Point Gully (page does not exist)"), [Long Point Gully](/w/index.php?title=Long_Point_Gully&action=edit&redlink=1 "Long Point Gully (page does not exist)"), [North Gully](/w/index.php?title=North_Gully&action=edit&redlink=1 "North Gully (page does not exist)"), [South Gully](/w/index.php?title=South_Gully&action=edit&redlink=1 "South Gully (page does not exist)"), [Cottonwood Gully](/w/index.php?title=Cottonwood_Gully&action=edit&redlink=1 "Cottonwood Gully (page does not exist)"), [North Mc Millian Creek](/w/index.php?title=North_Mc_Millian_Creek&action=edit&redlink=1 "North Mc Millian Creek (page does not exist)")

[/kəˈniːʃəs/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English") is a [Finger Lake](/wiki/Finger_Lakes "Finger Lakes") in [Livingston County](/wiki/Livingston_County,_New_York "Livingston County, New York") in western [New York](/wiki/New_York_(state) "New York (state)") state. The fifth smallest of eleven, it is located off [Interstate 390](/wiki/Interstate_390 "Interstate 390") about 15 miles (24 km) south of [Interstate 90](/wiki/Interstate_90 "Interstate 90").

["Conesus Lake"](https://edits.nationalmap.gov/apps/gaz-domestic/public/search/names/947265). *[Geographic Names Information System](/wiki/Geographic_Names_Information_System "Geographic Names Information System")*. [United States Geological Survey](/wiki/United_States_Geological_Survey "United States Geological Survey"), [United States Department of the Interior](/wiki/United_States_Department_of_the_Interior "United States Department of the Interior"). Retrieved June 8, 2015.

Crawford, Franklin (May 17, 2007). ["Cornell researchers confirm that deadly fish virus has spread to 19 species, threatening sport-fishing industry"](https://www.news.cornell.edu/stories/2007/05/deadly-fish-virus-vhsv-spreading-throughout-great-lakes-basin). *Cornell Chronicle*. Cornell University. Retrieved July 14, 2007.
["Viral Hemorrhagic Septicemia (VHS) in New York"](http://www.dec.ny.gov/animals/25328.html). New York State Department of Environmental Conservation. Retrieved August 8, 2009.
["About the CLA - Conesus Lake Association, Inc"](https://conesuslake.org/about-the-cla/). Retrieved 15 September 2025.

[![Map](https://maps.wikimedia.org/img/osm-intl,7,42.7752,-77.0169,350x200.png?lang=en&domain=en.wikipedia.org&title=Conesus_Lake&revid=1330468068&groups=_6ddcf2882044d18487dc53186329573fc78ffc2c&parser=legacy)](/wiki/Special:Map/7/42.7752/-77.0169/en)

The eleven Finger Lakes of New York State.  

This map: 

* [view](/wiki/Template:Finger_Lakes_map "Template:Finger Lakes map")
* [talk](/wiki/Template_talk:Finger_Lakes_map "Template talk:Finger Lakes map")
* [edit](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Template:Finger_Lakes_map "Special:EditPage/Template:Finger Lakes map")

**Conesus Lake** [/kəˈniːʃəs/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English") is a [Finger Lake](/wiki/Finger_Lakes "Finger Lakes") in [Livingston County](/wiki/Livingston_County,_New_York "Livingston County, New York") in western [New York](/wiki/New_York_(state) "New York (state)") state. The fifth smallest of eleven, it is located off [Interstate 390](/wiki/Interstate_390 "Interstate 390") about 15 miles (24 km) south of [Interstate 90](/wiki/Interstate_90 "Interstate 90").

## Description

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Conesus_Lake&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Description")]

Conesus Lake is eight miles (13 km) long, with a maximum depth of 66 feet (20 m). It flows south to north, from its inlet in the [Town of Conesus](/wiki/Conesus,_New_York "Conesus, New York") to its outlet, [Conesus Creek](/wiki/Conesus_Creek "Conesus Creek"), in Lakeville, a hamlet in the [Town of Livonia](/wiki/Livonia,_New_York "Livonia, New York") in [Livingston County](/wiki/Livingston_County,_New_York "Livingston County, New York"). Conesus Creek in turn flows into the [Genesee River](/wiki/Genesee_River "Genesee River") near [Avon](/wiki/Avon,_New_York "Avon, New York").

The first steamboat on Conesus Lake was named the "Jessie" launched July 1, 1874.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

In August 2006, the [New York State Department of Environmental Conservation](/wiki/New_York_State_Department_of_Environmental_Conservation "New York State Department of Environmental Conservation") confirmed that the lake was the first outside the contiguous [Great Lakes](/wiki/Great_Lakes "Great Lakes") waterways to be stricken with a new strain of [viral hemorrhagic septicemia](/wiki/Viral_hemorrhagic_septicemia "Viral hemorrhagic septicemia") (VHS), an infectious fish disease responsible for mass die-offs of many species, but not linked to any human health concerns.[[3]](#cite_note-Cornell_VHS-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4) The disease is spread between waterways through live or frozen [bait fish](/wiki/Bait_fish "Bait fish"), roe, contaminated fishing equipment or live water wells in boats.

## Recreation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Conesus_Lake&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Recreation")]

On July 3 of each year, the residents of the lake participate in a tradition called the "Ring of Fire", sponsored by the Conesus Lake Association.[[5]](#cite_note-5) Participants light [road flares](/wiki/Flare "Flare") around the lake and shoot off [fireworks](/wiki/Fireworks "Fireworks"). The festivities typically start at dusk, with approximately 10,000 flares lit at 10:00 PM.

When frozen over in the winter, Conesus Lake is used for snowmobiling and ice fishing.

### Fishing

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Conesus_Lake&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Fishing")]

Fish species present in the lake include [walleye](/wiki/Walleye "Walleye"), [northern pike](/wiki/Northern_pike "Northern pike"), [yellow perch](/wiki/Yellow_perch "Yellow perch"), [smallmouth bass](/wiki/Smallmouth_bass "Smallmouth bass"), [largemouth bass](/wiki/Largemouth_bass "Largemouth bass"), and [tiger muskie](/wiki/Tiger_muskie "Tiger muskie"). Access is available via state owned hard surface ramp off East Lake Road, a state owned hand launch access on Pebble Beach Road in the hamlet of Lakeville, a state owned hand launch access on [US-20A](/wiki/U.S._Route_20A_(New_York) "U.S. Route 20A (New York)") in the hamlet of Lakeville, or at a car top launch access on the south shore off [NY-256](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_256 "New York State Route 256").[[6]](#cite_note-6)

* [![Aerial view of Conesus Lake](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/0a/Aerial_view_of_Conesus_Lake.jpg/40px-Aerial_view_of_Conesus_Lake.jpg)](/wiki/File:Aerial_view_of_Conesus_Lake.jpg "Aerial view of Conesus Lake")

  Aerial view of Conesus Lake
